component_identification.content: Base box includes 84 oversized illustration cards, 1 score track board, 6 wooden rabbit pawns (one per color), 36 voting tokens (typically 6 per player color), and 1 rulebook. In the classic 3–6 player setup each player has their own color set and starts with 6 cards. Expansions are separate 84-card decks and do not change the core flow; Odyssey/other variants add components for larger player counts.

core_game_loop.content: Turn rhythm: active side reveals or frames information, teammates/opponents respond under that game’s communication limits, then resolve points or survival state and rotate responsibility. Teach this as a repeating three-step cycle: present signal, interpret signal, then resolve consequences. New players should always know whose decision matters right now and what hidden information cannot be shared. Most confusion comes from breaking communication restrictions or skipping reveal order, so keep sequence strict.

detailed_rules.content: Setup: each player takes a color set, places rabbit on 0, draws 6 cards. Storyteller chooses one hand card and gives a clue (word, phrase, sentence, sound, etc.). Other players each submit one card from hand that could fit that clue. Storyteller shuffles all submitted cards with their own and reveals them in numbered order. Non-storytellers secretly vote for the card they think belongs to storyteller; you cannot vote your own card. Scoring: if all or none guess correctly, storyteller gets 0 and all others get 2. Otherwise storyteller and correct guessers each get 3. Every non-storyteller also gets +1 for each vote received on their submitted decoy. Discard all played cards, draw back to six, pass storyteller clockwise. Important edge clarifications: clues may reference culture/private jokes, but table should agree on tone; ties in score are shared victory unless house rule says otherwise; if deck runs out during refill, finish round then end game.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Points come from three channels: storyteller success condition (usually +3 when some but not all guess), correct guessing of storyteller card (+3), and decoy attraction (+1 per vote on your card). End trigger is deck exhaustion during refill or reaching target score track in your edition; finish current round for fairness if needed. Highest total wins; ties share unless venue policy requires tiebreak.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) As storyteller, target partial clarity: one-third to two-thirds guess rate is ideal. 2) Watch each player’s imagination style; tailor clues to table, not abstract difficulty. 3) As non-storyteller, submit plausible-but-not-perfect cards to farm +1 vote points. 4) Track which reference domains (movies, poetry, memes) each player uses to decode future clues. 5) Late game, defend lead by safer guesses rather than flashy decoys.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 543; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
